title: Palicourea berteroana
taxonomy: Palicourea berteroana is classified within the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants. The genus Palicourea is a group of flowering plants native to the Americas, and within it, P. berteroana is a specific species. This species is further classified into the order Lamiales and the class Magnoliopsida. Further research may provide more detailed classification information about this plant species.

intro: Palicourea berteroana is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as a woody shrub or small tree native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which have been shown to help alleviate symptoms of fever, pain, and respiratory infections.
